etymology.description: phonetic 冎 (guā, "a type of ancient vessel") + semantic 口 (kǒu, "mouth") -> crooked; bent
etymology.interpretation: The character 咼 (guō, "crooked, bent") combines the phonetic component 冎 (guā, "a type of ancient vessel"), which suggests the sound, with the semantic component 口 (kǒu, "mouth"), indicating aspects related to opening or shape. Together, these components infer an image of something that does not maintain a straight line, thus producing the meaning "crooked, bent."
